frameworks.name: ultra-high field fMRI
frameworks.one_line: 7 T vs 3 T BOLD sensitivity compared across cortical and subcortical regions.

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making with delayed reward
frameworks.one_line: Random dot motion discrimination task with delayed monetary feedback as probe.

core_question: Does 7 T fMRI yield larger task-evoked response amplitudes than 3 T, and does the benefit depend on brain region?
key_assumptions: Task-evoked BOLD amplitude indexes regional neural engagement
Same participants scanned at both field strengths makes responses directly comparable
Random dot motion discrimination recruits known cortical and subcortical circuitry
manipulations: Field strength (3 T vs 7 T) within participants
key_findings: Field-strength dependence of decision-interval amplitudes differed across cortical, striatal, and brainstem regions
7 T vs 3 T benefit was generally larger in subcortical, particularly brainstem, structures
Stronger differential responses for easy vs hard decisions at 7 T in dopaminergic midbrain nuclei
interpretations: 7 T offers disproportionate sensitivity gains for small subcortical structures including dopaminergic midbrain
Enhanced easy-vs-hard contrast in midbrain at 7 T may reflect reward expectation
